text:
Epilepsy Centre 

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Patel  
St George’s University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust  
Blackshaw Road, London, SW17 0QT  

Dear Dr Patel,  

Daniel Morris, DOB: 14-11-1989, Hospital No: H392741 NHS No. 9075316248  
12 Willow Court, Croydon, CR0 6JP  

Diagnoses: 

Seizure disorder under review. 

I reviewed Daniel Morris in clinic today. He works as a warehouse picker, moving between tall racking in narrow aisles where lifting demands and pace vary by shift. He reports that since our last contact three months ago there have not been any episodes seen by others or described by himself suggestive of seizures. He has been attentive to sleep, hydration, and regular meals across early, late, and night shifts, and he has not required rescue medication at work or at home. There have been no new injuries, no unexplained tongue-biting, and no post-event confusion reported by colleagues or family. 

Medications: no change since last review. He remains on levetiracetam 1 g twice daily with good adherence and no notable side effects beyond occasional transient irritability that settles without intervention. He has not carried buccal midazolam recently, and none has been administered. 

Workplace considerations: he continues to use a buddy system when retrieving loads from higher bays, and his supervisor is aware of our ongoing review. He has maintained a simple log of any concerning events at work and has recorded none. He has not noticed any aura-like symptoms when moving rapidly between aisles or during heavier lifts. 

Examination today is unremarkable. Blood pressure and pulse are within normal limits, and neurological examination is non-focal. 

Plan: we will continue the current regimen and monitoring. He will keep his event diary and will inform us if there are any changes, particularly if colleagues witness episodes or if he experiences unexplained collapses or confusional spells. No further tests requested today. We will review again in six months, or sooner if events occur. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: since our last contact three months ago there have not been any episodes seen by others or described by himself suggestive of seizures

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "since our last contact three months ago there have not been any episodes ... suggestive of seizures." The clinic date is 02 October 2025, and the reference period is the last 3 months. This indicates zero seizures over a 3-month interval. Per the rules, seizure-free status requires at least 6 months; therefore, the correct normalized label is seizure free for 3 months.
seizure_frequency_number: since our last contact three months ago there have not been any episodes seen by others or described by himself suggestive of seizures

flaws.description: Gold (seizure free for 3 months) disagrees with Reference (seizure free for multiple month)
